name: Range Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering checking, savings, CDs, consumer loans, and trust services with a focus on local community banking.
description_long:
Range Bank is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank headquartered in Marquette, Michigan. The bank emphasizes its role as a local banking partner, positioning itself around community ownership and support for local growth. According to their website, they were voted 2024 Best Bank and operate with a philosophy that extends beyond traditional banking services.

Range Bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking products. Their deposit offerings include checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and ICS/CDARS services for multi-million dollar FDIC protection through IntraFi's network. On the lending side, they provide consumer loans for vehicles, boats, campers, and motorcycles with competitive rates and no prepayment penalties. They also offer business checking accounts tailored to different operational needs, trust and wealth management services, and specialized products like Range EarlyPay (early paycheck access up to two business days early) and Range SaveUp (round-up savings program).

Range Bank distinguishes itself through its community banking model and emphasis on personalized service. The bank highlights its local ownership structure and commitment to sparking community growth. Their offering of ICS and CDARS services through IntraFi provides customers with enhanced FDIC protection through a single banking relationship, which is a more sophisticated deposit insurance strategy typically found in community banks serving customers with substantial savings. The website emphasizes consumer education through fraud prevention resources and financial calculators.

As a regional community bank with a physical headquarters location in Marquette, Michigan, Range Bank serves a specific geographic market rather than offering nationwide digital-only services. Their online presence is functional but basic compared to larger national banks or neobanks. The website provides limited detail on specific product terms, rates, and eligibility requirements, instead directing customers to contact the bank or visit branches. The branch network appears limited based on available location data showing primarily the Marquette headquarters location. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ICS (Insured Cash Sweep) services through IntraFi
CDARS (Certificate of Deposit Account Registry Service) through IntraFi
Consumer loans for vehicles, boats, campers, and motorcycles
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full U.S. Government faith and credit
Offers ICS and CDARS services providing multi-million dollar FDIC protection through single banking relationship
Range EarlyPay feature allows access to paychecks up to two business days early
Range SaveUp automatic round-up savings program helps build savings without active effort
Consumer loans with competitive rates and no prepayment penalties for vehicles, boats, and recreational equipment
Dedicated trust and wealth management services available
Emphasis on community banking model with focus on local economic growth support
Free financial calculators and fraud prevention education resources available
cons: Limited geographic footprint appears concentrated in Michigan region; not a nationwide banking option
Website lacks specific interest rates, APRs, and detailed product terms requiring direct contact with bank
No clear information about digital banking capabilities, mobile app features, or online account opening process
Limited branch and ATM locations visible on website; may not be convenient for non-local customers
No transparent fee schedule or service charge information published on website
best_for: Michigan residents seeking community-focused local banking with personalized service
Consumers needing multi-million dollar FDIC protection for large savings through ICS/CDARS services
Individuals looking for consumer loans for vehicle, boat, or recreational equipment financing without prepayment penalties
Business owners seeking checking accounts with flexibility and community bank relationship
diagnosis: Range Bank is best for Michigan residents and customers with substantial savings seeking personalized community banking with enhanced FDIC protection through ICS/CDARS. The main caveat is their apparent limited geographic footprint and lack of transparent product details on their website, requiring direct contact with the bank for rates and specific terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
